description_long: This sculpture depicts a standing human figure, likely female, draped in classical attire with flowing garments that gather at the waist and fall in naturalistic folds. The figure's right arm is bent at the elbow and raised, while the left arm is extended outward, suggesting a gesture or interaction. The head is adorned with stylized hair, possibly tied back, and the facial features are softly rendered. The figure stands on a rectangular plinth, with one foot slightly forward, giving a sense of movement. The surface of the sculpture appears to be stone or a similar material, with a matte, weathered finish. The overall style is reminiscent of neoclassical or baroque garden statuary, often found in parks or urban plazas.
description: A classical-style stone sculpture of a draped female figure standing on a rectangular plinth, with one arm raised and the other extended outward. The surface is matte and weathered, with detailed folds in the garments.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the back of the figure's draped garments and hair, with the right arm bent and partially raised.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the extended left arm and the naturalistic folds of the dress, as well as the figure's forward stride.
description_view_2: Frontal view displaying the face, both arms (one raised, one extended), and the detailed drapery of the attire.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the bent right arm and the side profile of the figure's head and body.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture could be suited for an outdoor urban plaza, park, or garden.
